---
title: "Data Overview"
subtitle: "Redd Count Time Series by Core Area and Population"
date: today
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo    = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.align = "center"
)
```

```{r load}
library(here)
library(tidyverse)

source(here("Code", "MPVA_Functions1_4.R"))
source(here("Code", "PrepareData_MPVA1_2.R"))

# Build tidy redd count data frame
# n_in: nSites x nYears  (rows = sites, cols = years)
# LocalPops_Num: site -> population index
# CoreArea_Num:  site -> core area index (site-level)
pop_core_vec <- CoreArea_Num[match(seq_len(nPop), LocalPops_Num)]

df_redds <- as.data.frame(n_in) |>
  setNames(as.character(DatYears)) |>
  mutate(
    site     = row_number(),
    pop      = LocalPops_Num,
    pop_name = U_LocalPops_In[LocalPops_Num],
    core     = CoreArea_Num[match(LocalPops_Num, LocalPops_Num)],
    core_name = U_CoreAreas_In[core]
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = as.character(DatYears),
    names_to  = "year",
    values_to = "redds"
  ) |>
  mutate(year = as.integer(year))
```

Redd counts are organized by core area (tabs) and local population (panels).
Each point is an observed count from one survey site; the line connects
population-level totals across all sites in that population-year.
Missing years reflect surveys that were not conducted.

::: {.panel-tabset}

```{r core-tabs, results='asis'}
cores <- sort(unique(df_redds$core))

for (ca in cores) {

  core_label <- U_CoreAreas_In[ca]
  pops_in_core <- sort(unique(df_redds$pop[df_redds$core == ca]))
  n_pops <- length(pops_in_core)

  cat("\n## ", core_label, "\n\n", sep = "")

  # Summary stats for this core
  df_core <- df_redds |> filter(core == ca)
  n_obs   <- sum(!is.na(df_core$redds))
  yr_range <- range(df_core$year[!is.na(df_core$redds)])

  cat(
    sprintf(
      "**%d local populations** | **%d – %d** | **%d** site-year observations\n\n",
      n_pops, yr_range[1], yr_range[2], n_obs
    )
  )

  # Build population totals (sum across sites per pop-year)
  df_pop_total <- df_core |>
    group_by(pop_name, year) |>
    summarise(total = sum(redds, na.rm = TRUE),
              any_obs = any(!is.na(redds)),
              .groups = "drop") |>
    mutate(total = if_else(any_obs, total, NA_real_))

  # Wrap in a code chunk via knitr::knit_expand isn't needed —
  # we just print the ggplot directly
  p <- ggplot() +
    # Site-level raw counts as points
    geom_point(
      data = df_core |> filter(!is.na(redds)),
      aes(x = year, y = redds),
      alpha = 0.5, size = 1.5, color = "grey50"
    ) +
    # Population total as line
    geom_line(
      data = df_pop_total |> filter(!is.na(total)),
      aes(x = year, y = total),
      linewidth = 0.9, color = "steelblue"
    ) +
    geom_point(
      data = df_pop_total |> filter(!is.na(total)),
      aes(x = year, y = total),
      size = 2, color = "steelblue"
    ) +
    facet_wrap(~pop_name, scales = "free_y",
               ncol = min(3, n_pops)) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 5)) +
    labs(
      x       = "Year",
      y       = "Redd Count",
      caption = "Grey points = individual survey sites; blue line = population total"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
    theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold"))

  print(p)
  cat("\n\n")
}
```

:::
